pages.text: $(thing)Fermentation$() needs enough warmth and enough barrel humidity. It takes about $(item)2 Minecraft days$(). When it finishes, the tobacco becomes fermented and gains quality.
$(thing)Aging$() needs cool, dark storage with moderate humidity. It is slower and gentler than fermentation, but it can steadily improve decent tobacco over time.
Too much heat can spoil a batch. Extremely old tobacco can also go bad. Good barrel work rewards patience and stable conditions, not extremes.
